title: Not With My Cousin You Dont

jokes.text: I lost my head.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks Derek why he stopped kissing her
jokes.punchline: Double meaning - lost composure vs. literally losing one's head
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay pun, fairly predictable given romantic context

jokes.text: Derek, you'll get in. You're too smart. I wish you were dumber.
jokes.setup: Ashley discussing Derek getting into Stanford
jokes.punchline: Wishing intelligence away for selfish romantic reasons
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ashley's conflicted feelings, well-structured ironic wish

jokes.text: I'd have a boyfriend again.
jokes.setup: Following her wish that Derek was dumber
jokes.punchline: Revealing her selfish motivation - keeping him around
jokes.explanation: Natural follow-up that clarifies the joke's logic

jokes.text: Aw!
jokes.setup: Derek declares his love for Ashley
jokes.punchline: Will's exaggerated sentimental reaction to their romance
jokes.explanation: Sets up Will's character dynamic as the interrupting cousin

jokes.text: Look at you two. Almost a year apart and you two lovebirds haven't missed a beat.
jokes.setup: Will interrupting their romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Mock-romantic commentary with slight edge
jokes.explanation: Will establishing his role as the protective but sarcastic cousin

jokes.text: Although, these are the moments of your life.
jokes.setup: Will pretending to leave them alone
jokes.punchline: Reference to Kodak commercial slogan, treating their romance like a commercial
jokes.explanation: Smart pop culture reference that fits Will's character of commenting on situations

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, you're right. You guys are grown adults and I don't have no business disrespecting you like this.
jokes.setup: Will's fake apology for interrupting
jokes.punchline: Obvious insincerity while calling teenagers 'grown adults'
jokes.explanation: Good sarcasm delivery typical of Will's protective yet mocking attitude

jokes.text: Uh-uh-uh.
jokes.setup: Derek explaining romantic memorabilia
jokes.punchline: Will's disapproving finger wag at their romantic history
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat showing Will's protective instincts

jokes.text: Together?
jokes.setup: Derek suggesting they make love
jokes.punchline: Ashley's innocent clarification of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect innocent reaction that highlights Ashley's naivety in a very economical way

jokes.text: Yuck it up, Brit boy, but remember, he who laughs last...
jokes.setup: Carlton threatening Geoffrey after being mocked
jokes.punchline: Setting up the classic phrase
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for the physical comedy payoff that follows

jokes.text: Laughs best.
jokes.setup: Carlton completing his threat
jokes.punchline: Carlton presumably falls or gets shocked by the intercom
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff physical comedy with Carlton's hubris being punished

jokes.text: You know, I kind of remember him being taller.
jokes.setup: Carlton presumably on the ground after his accident
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry observation about Carlton's reduced stature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan that plays on Carlton's short stature, excellent economy

jokes.text: Really? I don't.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey's comment about Carlton being taller
jokes.punchline: Will implying Carlton was never tall to begin with
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up joke that extends the height gag with Will's typical mockery of Carlton

jokes.text: I'm gonna let that one slide, young man. Obviously, you've fallen in with a bad element.
jokes.setup: Will's height joke about Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton blaming Geoffrey for Will's behavior instead of addressing the insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Carlton's pompous way of deflecting insults

jokes.text: Me and my man, we call it the Hive. Because the honeys be swarming.
jokes.setup: Will describing ULA to Derek
jokes.punchline: Bee metaphor for attractive women at university
jokes.explanation: Typical Will slang/wordplay, fits his character's style of talking

jokes.text: That's disgusting. No. Actually, Carlton, that's a kiss. If you're lucky, one day you'll experience one.
jokes.setup: Carlton calling Derek and Ashley's kiss disgusting
jokes.punchline: Will mocking Carlton's lack of romantic experience
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback that plays on Carlton's inexperience, good structure

jokes.text: Very funny. By the way, Will, I just got a call from Oz...the Wizard said your brain is ready.
jokes.setup: Carlton responding to Will's kiss joke
jokes.punchline: Wizard of Oz reference calling Will brainless
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference comeback, shows Carlton can hold his own verbally

jokes.text: Really? Let me help you.
jokes.setup: Will saying he's watching Derek
jokes.punchline: Will presumably making exaggerated watching gestures or getting too close
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beat showing Will's confrontational attitude

jokes.text: I never liked the cut of that boy's jib. I'm not sure you should be admitting you've been looking at that boy's jib.
jokes.setup: Carlton using old-fashioned sailing expression
jokes.punchline: Will suggesting sexual connotation to Carlton's words
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that takes Carlton's formal language and gives it unintended meaning

jokes.text: Makeup? Keep away from that blue eye shadow.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking her dad about makeup as cover for sex question
jokes.punchline: Philip's awkward, unhelpful advice about specific makeup
jokes.explanation: Shows Philip's discomfort with teenage girl issues, awkward father moment

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Big-sister radar.
jokes.setup: Ashley approaching Hilary for advice
jokes.punchline: Hilary's self-aware recognition of sibling dynamics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Hilary's awareness despite her usual self-absorption

jokes.text: It's important, isn't it? I can tell. I have, like, a third sense about these things.
jokes.setup: Hilary claiming intuition about Ashley's problem
jokes.punchline: Saying 'third sense' instead of 'sixth sense'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary malapropism showing her ditziness while trying to be wise

jokes.text: Oh! To put it simply, he was a god. He had been held back three years so he was really buff.
jokes.setup: Hilary describing her first love Lawrence
jokes.punchline: Equating being held back in school with physical fitness
jokes.explanation: Great Hilary logic - connecting academic failure to physical attractiveness in her unique way

jokes.text: But long-distance relationships don't usually work out. I mean, look at the Little Mermaid.
jokes.setup: Hilary giving relationship advice to Ashley
jokes.punchline: Using Disney cartoon as relationship guidance
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hilary moment - using children's fairy tale as serious relationship advice, perfect character integration

jokes.text: I mean, she was stuck in the sea and he was stuck on land.
jokes.setup: Hilary's Little Mermaid reference
jokes.punchline: Taking the fairy tale metaphor literally and seriously
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd Little Mermaid logic, escalating the ridiculous comparison

jokes.text: Any regrets? Didn't I tell you he was buff? Hello.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking if Hilary regrets her first sexual experience
jokes.punchline: Hilary focusing only on the physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the 'buff' comment, shows Hilary's priorities and shallow focus

jokes.text: Hoo! Now I know how Mom felt when we had this talk.
jokes.setup: Hilary finishing her advice to Ashley about sex
jokes.punchline: Hilary recognizing she's now in the parental advice role
jokes.explanation: Nice moment of awareness from Hilary about generational cycles

jokes.text: What did Daddy say when you told him? Are you kidding? He still thinks I'm a virgin.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking about telling their father
jokes.punchline: Hilary revealing she's never told Philip the truth
jokes.explanation: Good insight into father-daughter dynamics and family secrets

jokes.text: Okay, what do you think? Nope.
jokes.setup: Ashley presumably showing outfit options
jokes.punchline: Keesha's immediate and definitive rejection
jokes.explanation: Simple friend reaction, economical but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Well, if you guys don't pick one, I'll be going on this date wearing a headband.
jokes.setup: Friends rejecting Ashley's outfit choices
jokes.punchline: Threat to wear just a headband as entire outfit
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist threat that shows Ashley's frustration with fashion choices

jokes.text: I'm sure Derek wouldn't mind.
jokes.setup: Ashley worrying about what to wear
jokes.punchline: Keesha implies Derek won't care about clothes
jokes.explanation: Standard innuendo about upcoming sexual encounter

jokes.text: When? If.
jokes.setup: Ashley saying when she and Derek make love
jokes.punchline: Ashley correcting herself from certainty to possibility
jokes.explanation: Nice moment showing Ashley's uncertainty beneath her confident exterior, very economical

jokes.text: Oh, there's a surprise.
jokes.setup: Learning Carlton's room is quiet
jokes.punchline: Will's sarcastic comment about Carlton's lack of social activity
jokes.explanation: Standard Will mockery of Carlton's social life, predictable but in character

jokes.text: I wonder how I'd look if I shaved my entire body.
jokes.setup: Carlton's intercom picking up Geoffrey's private thoughts
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's bizarre personal grooming contemplation
jokes.explanation: Unexpected and disturbing glimpse into Geoffrey's private thoughts, good cringe comedy

jokes.text: Keesha, it's not 'doing it.' It's making love. And, yes, I think we're gonna do it tonight.
jokes.setup: Ashley correcting friend's crude language about sex
jokes.punchline: Immediately using the same crude language herself
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure showing the gap between Ashley's idealistic and practical attitudes

jokes.text: Oh, my God. What are we gonna do?
jokes.setup: Carlton and Will overhearing Ashley's sex plans
jokes.punchline: Carlton's panicked overreaction to normal teenage behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character reaction showing Carlton's prudish, protective nature

jokes.text: We'll make her watch movies. Lots of movies. Doris Day movies. Now, there's a good wholesome role model.
jokes.setup: Carlton planning to stop Ashley
jokes.punchline: Suggesting 1950s actress as modern sexual education
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton solution - outdated, impractical, and completely missing the point

jokes.text: Que sera sera, whatever will be, will be
jokes.setup: Carlton suggesting Doris Day as role model
jokes.punchline: Will singing Doris Day's famous song
jokes.explanation: Natural callback to the Doris Day reference, shows Will knows the reference

jokes.text: Wait a minute. 'Whatever will be, will be'? What kind of devil-may-care message is that?
jokes.setup: Will singing the innocent Doris Day song
jokes.punchline: Suddenly realizing the song promotes dangerous philosophy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of innocent song lyrics as dangerous moral guidance, great escalation

jokes.text: The slut.
jokes.setup: Will's realization about Doris Day's message
jokes.punchline: Calling wholesome 1950s icon a slut based on song lyrics
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic escalation with maximum economy - one word that completely subverts wholesome image

jokes.text: Look, you need to calm down, all right? Look, we need to find a Yellow Pages. I'm calling a nunnery. It's late, so I'll need a 24-hour one.
jokes.setup: Carlton's panic about Ashley's situation
jokes.punchline: Carlton wanting to find a 24-hour nunnery like it's a business service
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd escalation treating religious institutions like commercial services, perfect Carlton logic

jokes.text: Carlton, man, we ain't even Catholic. Oh! Would you have an open mind?
jokes.setup: Will pointing out they're not Catholic
jokes.punchline: Carlton asking Will to be open-minded about changing religions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the nunnery joke, Carlton's desperation overriding religious logic

jokes.text: This is all your fault. 'Ashley could never say no to you.' I told you I didn't like the cut of his jib.
jokes.setup: Carlton blaming Will for the situation
jokes.punchline: Bringing back the 'jib' phrase and earlier blame
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier jib joke and Will's encouragement, shows Carlton's panic-driven logic

jokes.text: Need I remind you there are demons out there that go by the name of: Sexually transmitted diseases, risk of pregnancy...improper use of a condom?
jokes.setup: Carlton arguing they need to take action
jokes.punchline: Dramatic presentation of standard sex education topics as 'demons'
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton dramatic escalation, treating practical concerns as supernatural threats

jokes.text: Thank God you're alive.
jokes.setup: Derek showing up despite Will's fake accident story
jokes.punchline: Carlton's dramatic relief about non-existent accident
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Will's lie, Carlton's dramatic sincerity about fake crisis

jokes.text: So, Derek, you know about football? Yeah. Yeah, you do? Or, yeah, you don't?
jokes.setup: Carlton trying to start intimidating conversation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's confusion making the interrogation incompetent
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Carlton's nervousness ruining their intimidation attempt

jokes.text: You're in a visitor's ballpark and if you do score...the angry fans might not be able to be held responsible for their actions. And it's bat night.
jokes.setup: Will's extended baseball metaphor about Derek and Ashley
jokes.punchline: Adding 'bat night' as extra threat
jokes.explanation: Good extended metaphor with escalating threat, 'bat night' is perfect absurd detail

jokes.text: All right, are you two in trouble with a bookie or something?
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton's confusing sports metaphors
jokes.punchline: Derek's logical confusion about their bizarre behavior
jokes.explanation: Good reaction that grounds the absurd sports metaphors in reality, Derek as straight man

jokes.text: You're playing chess, your rook has been blindsided by the queen's gambit. You can play honorably, leave your bishop at home but protect your queen's virtue...or you can take the sleazy road and castle your king and go right for the mate. And it's bat night.
jokes.setup: Carlton trying his own metaphorical threat
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical chess metaphor ending with callback to 'bat night'
jokes.explanation: Carlton's attempt to match Will's metaphor style, absurd chess terminology with bat night callback

jokes.text: Do I make myself clear?
jokes.setup: Carlton's incomprehensible chess metaphor
jokes.punchline: Carlton asking if his confusing speech was clear
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic punctuation to Carlton's confusing speech, good economy and character work

jokes.text: Ashley, now spying, that's such an ugly word. We were roaming.
jokes.setup: Ashley accusing them of spying
jokes.punchline: Will's euphemistic defense using technical intercom term
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay defense, Will trying to minimize their violation of privacy

jokes.text: You know, I would expect this from Carlton...but not you.
jokes.setup: Ashley expressing disappointment in Will and Carlton
jokes.punchline: Singling out Carlton as the expected problem while Will is the disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good character distinction showing family dynamics and Carlton's reputation

jokes.text: Uh...
jokes.setup: Ashley saying she expected this behavior from Carlton
jokes.punchline: Carlton's awkward non-response to being called predictably problematic
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat showing Carlton's embarrassment at his reputation

jokes.text: Are you? Sure. Uh...I guess.
jokes.setup: Ashley asking if Derek is ready for sex
jokes.punchline: Derek's decreasing confidence from 'sure' to 'I guess'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Derek's uncertainty beneath his bravado

jokes.text: So, heh, what do you wanna do now? I don't know. Miniature golf? Let's do it. Golf.
jokes.setup: The couple deciding what to do instead of having sex
jokes.punchline: Choosing the most innocent, childlike activity possible
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast - from considering sex to miniature golf, shows their youth

jokes.text: Back off, Will. You telling me to back off, girl? Hold up. Now, this is me.
jokes.setup: Ashley telling Will to back off
jokes.punchline: Will's indignant response asserting their close relationship
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the emotional conversation, Will asserting their bond

jokes.text: You remember the first time I asked you about sex? You panicked then too. I did not. Yes, you did. You said that you needed to have a license to have sex...and that I couldn't even take the test until I was 35.
jokes.setup: Ashley remembering their first sex talk
jokes.punchline: Will's ridiculous explanation about needing a license for sex
jokes.explanation: Great character backstory that's both funny and touching, shows their relationship history

jokes.text: That's true.
jokes.setup: Ashley recounting Will's sex license story
jokes.punchline: Will still standing by his ridiculous advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that extends the joke, Will doubling down on his absurd advice

jokes.text: That was so beautiful.
jokes.setup: Will and Ashley's emotional reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sentimental reaction to their cousin bonding
jokes.explanation: Typical Carlton sentimental reaction that ends the episode on a light note after the emotional moment

tmdb_overview: Freakin' out! On the home intercom, Will and Carlton overhear Ashley talking about sex.
